description.task: Build a custom application properties reader in Java that loads key-value pairs from a properties string and supports default values — simulating Spring Boot's application.properties.
instructions: Implement load() to split content by newlines, skip lines starting with '#' or blank lines, and parse 'key=value' pairs.
Implement all three getProperty variants as described.
In Main, print each property on its own line with the format 'key: value'.
Print app.version with default '1.0.0' and server.port as int.
description.hint: Spring Boot's application.properties uses the same pattern. You can also use YAML (application.yml) for hierarchical configuration.
description.expectedOutput:
server.port: 8080
server.name: MyApp
spring.datasource.url: jdbc:postgresql://localhost/mydb
app.version: 1.0.0 (default)
port as int: 8080

description.task: Implement a multi-profile properties system that merges a base application.properties with a profile-specific override file (like application-dev.properties).
instructions: Implement get() to check override first, then base, returning null if not found in either.
Implement getEffectiveConfig() by copying base then putting all override entries (override wins).
Run Main as-is — do not change Main.
Output should show all keys, with overridden values from dev profile.
description.hint: Spring Boot loads application-{profile}.properties and merges it with application.properties. Profile-specific values always take precedence.
description.expectedOutput:
Effective configuration (dev profile):
  server.port = 8080
  app.name = MyApp
  logging.level = DEBUG
  db.url = jdbc:postgresql://localhost/devdb
  debug.enabled = true

description.task: Simulate Spring Boot's @ConfigurationProperties by building a binder that maps flat key-value properties into a structured configuration class using reflection.
description.context: Spring Boot's @ConfigurationProperties binds properties like 'server.port=8080' to a Java object field 'serverPort'. This eliminates boilerplate getter calls.
instructions: Implement getters for port, host, maxConnections in ServerConfig.

In ConfigBinder.bind(), iterate all declared fields of target. For fields annotated with @Value, get the key, look up in props, and set the field value (use Integer.parseInt for int fields, setAccessible(true)).
Output should show the bound ServerConfig.
description.hint: Spring's @ConfigurationProperties uses a similar binding mechanism. For complex types, it supports nested objects and relaxed binding (server.port = serverPort).
